title: Aurora expert captures rare pulsating northern lights in remarkable detail: 'One of the most profound sightings of my career'
summary: An expert captured rare pulsating northern lights in Arctic Norway during a powerful geomagnetic storm lasting nearly three hours. The display was described as one of the most profound sightings of the expert's career.

title: Scientists learn how much baby stars in Orion weigh — by watching their dance moves
summary: Scientists have directly measured the masses of young binary stars in Orion's star-forming gas clouds using radio astronomy. This confirms the accuracy of current theoretical models.

title: Could the moon ever be blockaded? Experts predict cislunar space could be the next Strait of Hormuz
summary: Experts warn that cislunar space, the region between Earth and the Moon, could become a future geopolitical flashpoint akin to the Strait of Hormuz. This scenario is being compared to ongoing military tensions involving Iran.

title: Did decaying dark matter help create the universe's first supermassive black holes?
summary: Decaying dark matter may have contributed to the formation of the universe's first supermassive black holes, as suggested by observations from the James Webb Space Telescope.

title: The 1st 'Alien: Isolation 2' teaser is here, and the setting looks eerily familiar
summary: The first teaser for 'Alien: Isolation 2' has been released, featuring a familiar setting that evokes a dark and intense atmosphere.

title: All 4 original Alien movie special editions are now on HBO Max; here's how they differ from the theatrical cuts
summary: HBO Max has added all four original Alien movie special editions, highlighting differences between theatrical and director's cuts. The additions coincide with Alien Day celebrations.

title: Watch comet PANSTARRS 'switch on' its second tail as it makes closest approach to Earth
summary: Comet C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS made its closest approach to Earth at 45 million miles, revealing a newly activated ion tail. This event highlights the dynamic behavior of comets as they interact with solar radiation.

title: SpaceX launches its 50th mission of the year, sends 25 Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its 50th mission of the year, deploying 25 Starlink satellites into orbit from California. This milestone underscores the company's rapid launch cadence in 2024.

title: We went hands-on with 'The Expanse: Osiris Reborn', and it's a hard sci-fi 'Mass Effect' successor with show-stealing zero-G sections
summary: Owlcat Games' 'The Expanse: Osiris Reborn' is a hard sci-fi successor to 'Mass Effect' with impressive zero-G sections. The game has been praised for its engaging gameplay and faithful adaptation of the source material.

title: NASA’s X-59 Gets Freedom 250 Logo
summary: NASA's X-59 aircraft received a special livery to celebrate the 250th anniversary of U.S. independence, featuring the 'Freedom 250' logo. The aircraft is part of NASA's Quesst mission to demonstrate quiet supersonic flight.

title: NASA’s Perseverance, Curiosity Panoramas Capture Two Sides of Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity and Perseverance rovers have captured 360-degree panoramas of Mars, revealing details about the planet's formation, watery past, and potential for life. The rovers are exploring regions 2,345 miles apart, offering complementary scientific insights.

title: You Can Help Humans Thrive in Space
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission marked the first crewed deep-space flight since 1972, sending four astronauts around the moon. The public can contribute to human space exploration through NASA's citizen science initiatives.

title: Volunteers Help NASA Astronauts Record Lunar Flashes
summary: NASA's Artemis II astronauts observed lunar flashes caused by meteoroid impacts while volunteers with the Impact Flash project recorded the events from Earth using telescopes.

title: I Am Artemis: Peter Rossoni
summary: Peter Rossoni, Orion Artemis II Optical Communications System flight manager, reflects on his journey from watching Apollo missions as a child to participating in NASA's Artemis II mission in April 2026. He now plays a key role in enabling communications for astronauts journeying around the Moon.

title: The Day of the Trifid Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a detailed image of the Trifid Nebula, 5,000 light-years from Earth, to celebrate the mission's 36th launch anniversary in April 2026.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Missouri Students
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack Hathaway will answer STEM questions from Missouri students during a live call from the International Space Station on April 30. The event will be streamed on NASA's YouTube channel.

title: NASA Celebrates Decade of University Innovation in Aeronautics
summary: NASA's University Leadership Initiative (ULI) celebrates a decade of fostering aeronautical innovation and workforce development, supporting over 1,100 students across 100 schools.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent successful Artemis II mission, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return, while ULA's Atlas V and Arianespace's Ariane 6 are also involved in the launches.

title: SpaceX scrubs launch of Falcon Heavy on Monday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX postponed the launch of its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. The scrub marks a delay in the rocket's return to flight.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were highlighted in a report by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the recent CRS NG-24 mission.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship program at Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year as the first Block 3 flight approaches.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from the NASA Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and progress of developing new spacesuits for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: A busy week in the space industry is expected with seven missions launching from four countries using five different launch vehicles. The missions include GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the New Glenn launch. The incident follows Blue Origin's recent milestone of landing a reused first-stage booster.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or spacecraft. This innovation could significantly improve space operations and safety.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes in-person and online events in Paris and April-May 2026.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for its navigation system. The exact location or context of this achievement is not specified in the provided text.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution videos of the Sun's corona. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, specifically highlighting improvements in directional control systems. This innovation is crucial for enhancing mission precision and efficiency in space exploration.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team tested ultralight graphene aerogels with lasers during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, observing significant propulsion in microgravity. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, demonstrated that light propulsion could steer solar sails or adjust satellite positions.

title: Isaacman Continues to Defend Budget Cuts to Congress
summary: Isaacman defended budget cuts to Congress while emphasizing NASA's top goal of accelerating the US return to the Moon. The hearing provided further details on the agency's objectives.

title: True Anomaly Closes $650M Series D
summary: True Anomaly, a four-year-old space company, raised $650 million in a Series D funding round. The company also announced its Golden Dome award for space-based interceptor technology.

title: Meta Secures Overview Energy Space Solar Power Capacity
summary: Meta has secured an agreement with Overview Energy for up to 1 GW of power capacity through a planned solar power-beaming satellite system. This deal highlights Meta's investment in innovative energy solutions for its operations.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon has selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI Tech program, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion in total. This initiative aims to enhance space-based capabilities for national defense.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to improve scalability and reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The initiative is part of Astra's strategy to enhance its rocket technology.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Former President Trump has appointed two space industry executives to key military space roles. The nominees require Senate confirmation before assuming their positions.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, potentially powering future moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: A cost analysis compares the build costs of an orbital data center with a terrestrial one, including opportunity costs. The article is published by Payload Pro.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has secured €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional backing from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have pledged to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, citing concerns that the cuts would hinder the agency's ability to meet the directives set by President Trump. This marks another instance of bipartisan pushback against reduced funding for NASA.

title: ULA Launches 29 Satellites in 6th Mission for Amazon Leo
summary: United Launch Alliance (ULA) successfully launched 29 satellites for Amazon's Leo constellation using an Atlas V rocket. This marks ULA's sixth mission supporting Amazon's satellite network.

title: Gatehouse Satcom and Rohde & Schwarz Partner on 5GN NTN Testing
summary: Gatehouse Satcom and Rohde & Schwarz have partnered to collaborate on 5G non-terrestrial network (NTN) testing, leveraging Gatehouse's software expertise and Rohde & Schwarz's testing capabilities.

title: HawkEye 360 Files to Go Public, Reports $99M in Revenue in 2025
summary: HawkEye 360, a signals intelligence company, plans to go public in 2024 with a road show for an IPO. The company reported $99 million in revenue for 2025.

title: Pakistan Adds to EO Fleet of Satellites
summary: Pakistan launched the EO-3 satellite, expanding its Earth Observation (EO) fleet. The Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (SUPARCO) conducted the launch.

title: Space Force Awards $3.2 Billion to 12 Companies for Space-Based Interceptor Work
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded contracts totaling up to $3.2 billion to 12 companies for developing space-based interceptor technologies under the Golden Dome initiative. This move aims to enhance space defense capabilities.

title: GomSpace to Establish New Ukrainian Joint Venture for Sovereign Comms
summary: GomSpace, a Danish satellite company, is establishing a new joint venture in Ukraine to develop sovereign satellite communication capabilities. The partnership with Ukrainian firm Stetman aims to enhance Ukraine's satellite infrastructure.

title: Rocket Lab Introduces Next-Generation Star Tracker Component
summary: Rocket Lab has launched a next-generation star tracker component, the High-Performance Star Tracker (ST-HP), designed for extended spacecraft missions. This expands Rocket Lab's offerings in space components.

title: FCC Rejects Requests for Sharing in MSS Spectrum, Maintaining Exclusive Rights
summary: The FCC rejected SpaceX and other operators' requests to share MSS spectrum bands, maintaining exclusive licensing rights. The decision was formalized in an order issued on Thursday.

title: SDA’s Need for Speed Pushes Startups for Results
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is pausing launches until May to address technology hurdles, supply chain challenges, and balance speed with results. Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo emphasized the need for startups to deliver amid these strategic delays.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to deploy teleport sites at Equinx's data centers for its Meridian constellation. This partnership aims to support the ground infrastructure for SpinLaunch's space-based services.
